opp.name: Cavendish Farms S2P - Phase I
opp.next_step: 5/26MF - update from they purchased Medius for Intake and AP last September. Have emailed Tyson Woods, Procurement Director to get a sense on whether this is a live opp or if the Medius purchase negates the need for a Zycus solution. 5/25MF - emailing attendees for the SCC conference, Adrian Mallett, (buyer) responded they purchased a P2P system last year. Have reached out to several others including Harshad to see if he can get any intel from Deloitte on whether this means our opp is nullified. 2.13.26 KC: Invited Ahmed to Pinnacle a 2nd time Rescheduling meeting for week of 2.16 1.19.26 KC: Meeting rescheduled for 2.11 @ 11am PST, 1
opp.ais_why:
• Kevin walked Affany through Zycus's agentic AI launch materials and use cases, with the transcript noting plans to 'leverage agentic AI to identify cost savings and workflow efficiencies in warehousing and transportation.'
• Customer is running a Control Tower / data-lake program with Deloitte and confirmed AI and automation are in demand planning, though production and logistics remain manual - receptive but not yet AI-led.
• Two substantive recorded calls plus monthly cadence; AI is a Zycus-led narrative the customer is open to, not a Cavendish-driven mandate, keeping confidence Medium.

sources: SF Next_Step__c and Next_Step_History__c (references Medius purchase, manual processes)
Avoma discovery call 16 September 2025: Ahmed Affany named supplier management, risk management, and 3PL invoice automation as high-value areas
Avoma call 17 November 2025: Ahmed confirmed warehousing invoice processing is manual via email, no standardization, very messy
metrics.narrative: Ahmed Affany articulated operational pain on the 16 September 2025 discovery call: supplier management and risk management capabilities are insufficient, 3PL invoice processing is highly manual and error-prone, spend analysis and KPI tracking are fragmented across warehouses with each site using different models. On the 17 November 2025 follow-up Ahmed confirmed warehousing invoices flow by email only without standardization, creating inefficiencies and accuracy challenges. Julie Michaud (Analyst, Freight Financials) expressed interest in ROI modeling using internal data rather than industry averages. However, no quantified business case was articulated by the buyer: no cost savings target, no headcount impact, no cycle-time reduction, no error-rate baseline. The pain is confirmed and specific; the quantified value case is missing. Zycus committed to deliver an ROI model using Cavendish data (action item 16 September and 17 November calls) but no evidence the buyer completed the data sheet or reviewed the model. Gap: quantify the manual invoice pain (hours, error cost, FTE load) and the supplier-risk exposure (supplier count, risk incidents, compliance gaps) to build the business case.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Ahmed Affany, MBA, CPIM (Director, Supply Chain Logistics) marked Decision Maker
Avoma call 16 September 2025: Ahmed initiated call, articulated pain, invited Julie and Remy, requested use cases and deeper-dive meeting
Avoma call 17 November 2025: Ahmed scheduled update, projected mid-Q2 2026 decision, requested roadmap and ROI data, requested NDA, stated 'I'm a big fan of this'
SF LastActivityDate 23 March 2026; last buyer call 11 February 2026 (touchpoint 5, not recorded); 135 days dark since
SF Next_Step__c 26 May 2026: rep reached out to Ahmed and others; no buyer reply
champion.narrative: Ahmed Affany (Director, Supply Chain Logistics, jdirving.com domain, named Decision Maker contact role) is the single champion. He initiated the 16 September 2025 discovery call through Merlin (Zycus BD), articulated clear pain (supplier/risk management, 3PL invoice automation, spend fragmentation), and actively drove the agenda on both recorded calls. On 16 September he requested specific use cases, invited Julie Michaud (Analyst, Freight Financials) and Remy Maye (Manager, Transportation) to the call to broaden the evaluation, and committed to schedule a deeper-dive meeting with optimization and warehousing stakeholders. On 17 November he proactively scheduled a status update, confirmed the budget cycle was nearly complete, projected a mid-Q2 2026 decision target, requested an implementation roadmap and ROI data sheet, and asked for an NDA. He described Zycus solutions as offering '100% value' and 'opportunity' for Cavendish, demonstrating genuine advocacy. However, Ahmed has no access to the Economic Buyer (none identified), has not introduced Zycus to the TMO office or CFO, and has not multi-threaded beyond Julie and Remy (both analysts/managers, not executives). Since 11 February 2026 (the last scheduled touchpoint) the relationship has gone completely dark: 135 days with no buyer reply to any Zycus outreach, including the critical 25-26 May 2026 inquiry about whether the Medius purchase nullifies the Zycus opportunity. Champion status is now AT RISK: Ahmed may have deprioritized Zycus after Medius went live, or internal priorities shifted, or he is waiting for budget/resource clarity and has no urgency to re-engage. The absence of multi-threading and EB access means the deal depends entirely on one contact who has been silent for 135 days. Gap: re-engage Ahmed directly (email and phone), escalate to the deal owner's manager for an executive check-in if Ahmed remains unresponsive, and push for introductions to the TMO office and the VP-level budget holder.

sources: SF Next_Step__c 26 May 2026: Medius purchased September 2025 for Intake and AP; Adrian Mallett confirmed P2P purchase; rep reached out to clarify if Zycus opportunity is nullified
Avoma call 17 November 2025: Ahmed confirmed Medius EDI procurement system implemented weeks ago; warehousing invoice standardization still pending
SF Products__c: iSupplier, eInvoicing, Merlin Intake overlap with Medius P2P scope
competition.narrative: Medius is the primary competitive threat and may have eliminated the Zycus opportunity entirely. Per Next_Step__c dated 26 May 2026, Cavendish purchased Medius for Intake and AP in September 2025 (weeks after the initial Zycus discovery call on 16 September 2025). On 17 November 2025 Ahmed confirmed on the call that 'Medius EDI procurement system implemented weeks ago,' which aligns with the September purchase. Adrian Mallett, a buyer contact from the SCC conference, told the Zycus rep in May 2026 that Cavendish 'purchased a P2P system last year,' further corroborating the Medius deployment. The rep reached out 25-26 May 2026 to the Procurement Director and to Harshad (Deloitte contact) to determine whether the Medius purchase negates the need for Zycus, but received no reply from any buyer contact. Medius directly competes with Zycus iSupplier, eInvoicing, and Merlin Intake modules listed in Products__c. However, on 17 November Ahmed stated that 'warehousing invoice standardization still pending' even after Medius went live, suggesting Medius may cover only procurement (EDI, AP) and not the 3PL warehousing invoice automation use case Ahmed identified as a high-value pain point. The competitive position is highly ambiguous: Medius may have won intake and AP, leaving warehousing and supplier/risk management open for Zycus, OR the buyer may view Medius as the full P2P platform and Zycus as redundant. With 95 days of silence since the last buyer activity, the deal may be lost to Medius by default. No other competitors (Coupa, SAP Ariba, GEP, Jaggaer, etc.) were named on any call or in any Salesforce field. Wedge if the deal is still live: position Zycus as the specialist for 3PL warehouse invoice automation, supplier risk, and agentic AI cost-savings discovery—capabilities Medius does not lead in—and co-exist with Medius rather than replace it.

sources: Avoma call 16 September 2025: Ahmed named supplier management, risk, 3PL invoice automation, fragmented spend analysis, and manual KPI tracking as pain points; Julie confirmed freight financials and ROI modeling interest
Avoma call 17 November 2025: Ahmed confirmed warehousing invoice processing is manual via email, no standardization, very messy; Medius implemented for EDI procurement but warehousing still pending
SF Next_Step__c references manual processes and need for automation
identify_pain.narrative: Ahmed Affany articulated specific, operational pain on 16 September 2025: supplier management and risk management capabilities are currently insufficient and identified as high-value areas for Zycus support; 3PL invoice processing is highly manual, creating inefficiencies and accuracy challenges; spend analysis and KPI tracking are fragmented across warehouses with each site using different models and manual reporting. On 17 November he elaborated: warehousing invoices flow by email only, requiring an integration layer for automated capture; existing processes are 'very messy,' 'manual,' and 'error-prone' when tracking accessorials and categories. He also confirmed Medius was implemented just weeks before the 17 November call for EDI procurement but that warehousing invoice standardization is still pending. Julie Michaud confirmed on 16 September that freight financials (budgeting, forecasting, network optimization) are within her scope and that ROI modeling using internal data (not industry averages) would be valuable for business-case support. Pain is confirmed, specific, and owned by Ahmed (Supply Chain) and Julie (Freight Finance). However, the pain has NOT been quantified: no baseline error rate, no manual hours per week, no cost per invoice, no supplier-risk incident count. The gap between confirmed pain and quantified pain is the missing business case.

sources: Avoma call 17 November 2025: Ahmed requested NDA before sharing ROI data; Kevin committed to check for existing NDA and send one
No subsequent evidence of NDA execution or ROI data sheet return
SF Next_Step__c and task history contain no contracting or legal activity
paper_process.narrative: On 17 November 2025 Ahmed requested an NDA, stating legal would need to review it before sharing ROI data. Kevin Chipotle (Zycus RD) committed to check for an existing NDA and send one if needed (action item 17 November call). However, there is no evidence in Next_Step__c, tasks, or the call manifest that the NDA was executed or that the ROI data sheet was returned. Contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, Order Form, legal review, InfoSec, procurement channel) were never discussed. Deloitte's role in integration was confirmed, but whether they also facilitate contracting or procurement is unclear. No current contract expiration date, no SI/partner contracting role, no legal or InfoSec milestone is on record. The paper process is entirely unaddressed. Gap: confirm NDA status, clarify Deloitte's role in contracting and procurement, and map the legal and InfoSec review steps required by Cavendish (J.D. Irving parent company may impose additional approval layers).

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: zero Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor roles on record
Avoma call 17 November 2025: Ahmed mentioned TMO office and business-case requirement but did not name the budget holder
Avoma call 16 September 2025: Julie Michaud (Analyst) joined for ROI discussion, not commercial authority
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer identified. Budget authority and commercial approvals were never discussed on the recorded calls. Ahmed Affany (Director, Supply Chain Logistics) is the primary contact and stated on 17 November 2025 that cross-functional investments require a business case presented through the TMO (Technology Management Office), but he did not name the budget holder or CFO sponsor. Julie Michaud (Analyst, Freight Financials) joined the 16 September call and confirmed she would be involved in ROI assessment, but her role is analytical, not commercial. On 17 November Ahmed projected a decision timeframe of mid-Q2 2026 contingent on IT and business hours and ROI, implying a committee-level approval, but no EB or CFO was named or engaged. The deal is 284 days old, 112 days to close, and no pricing or commercial conversation has occurred. Gap: map the TMO office, identify the CFO or VP who controls the S2P transformation budget, and secure an EB sponsor call. The absence of an EB at this stage is a fundamental blocker.

sources: Avoma call 16 September 2025: Ahmed described TMO office for cross-functional investments and division autonomy for single-function tools
Avoma call 17 November 2025: Ahmed confirmed budgeting nearly complete, mid-Q2 2026 decision target, Deloitte involvement required for integration
SF Next_Step__c 26 May 2026: rep reached out to clarify deal status post-Medius purchase; no buyer reply
SF LastActivityDate 23 March 2026, 95 days ago; deal dark since
decision_process.narrative: Ahmed Affany described the approval process on 16 September 2025: cross-functional (multi-company) investments require a business case submitted through the TMO office; division-specific investments (warehousing, transportation) are handled within the division but coordinated collaboratively with other J.D. Irving companies. On 17 November he confirmed the budgeting cycle was nearly complete (version one finished, version two minor tweaks), and he projected a decision timeframe of mid-Q2 2026 contingent on IT and business resource hours and ROI analysis. He also confirmed Deloitte is deeply involved in the transformation journey (data lake, hydration, use-case support, integration) and would need to be engaged for any Zycus implementation. However, no formal procurement process, no RFP timeline, no shortlist, no approval chain (CFO, CIO, VP), and no selection committee were named. The process is described in principle but not in motion. The deal has been dark for 95 days, and the last attempt to re-engage (25-26 May 2026) received no buyer reply, so the process may be on hold or the opportunity nullified by the Medius purchase. Gap: confirm the deal is still live, map the actual approval chain (TMO office members, CFO, CIO), and establish a mutual close plan with milestone dates.

sources: Avoma call 16 September 2025: Ahmed named supplier management, risk, 3PL invoice automation, data-lake integration, and ROI modeling as priorities

Avoma call 17 November 2025: Ahmed requested specific use cases for supplier risk, invoicing, spend analysis, warehouse management; confirmed use-case-by-use-case evaluation approach and Deloitte involvement in data infrastructure
SF Next_Step__c 26 May 2026: rep reached out to clarify if Medius purchase (intake and AP) negates the Zycus opportunity
decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer articulated specific evaluation criteria on the 16 September 2025 discovery call: strengthening supplier management, automating risk management, unifying 3PL invoice processing through a control-tower approach, enabling ROI modeling with internal data (not industry averages), and integrating with the data lake and hydration initiatives underway with the relevant stakeholder confirmed Cavendish has engaged Gartner for 10 years and established a TMO office to advance data governance, building a data lake, and introducing AI in demand planning. On 17 November Ahmed confirmed the use-case-by-use-case approach and requested tailored industry use cases for supplier risk, invoicing, spend analysis, and warehouse management (particularly 3PL integration). Zycus committed to deliver one to two use cases per module (action item both calls). However, there is no evidence of a formal RFP, a scoring rubric, a documented BRD, or a competitive bake-off. The criteria are directional and pain-led, not formalized into a decision framework. Gap: co-author a written evaluation plan with Ahmed, document the must-have vs nice-to-have criteria, and confirm whether Medius (purchased September 2025) now covers intake and AP, reducing the scope for Zycus or eliminating the need entirely.

ai_fit_signal.summary: Ahmed confirmed on 16 September 2025 that Cavendish has engaged Gartner for 10 years, established a TMO office, is building a data lake and data hydration initiatives, and has introduced AI in demand planning. However, he stated logistics and warehousing AI are 'still a little bit off site.' Interest in automation is high (3PL invoice automation, spend analysis, KPI tracking), but AI appetite is selective and use-case-driven, not enterprise-wide. No AIS score on record.

Anchor the AI narrative to the buyer's existing data-lake and automation initiatives (Gartner, TMO office, demand-planning AI). Raise deal value and urgency.

best_practice_check.summary: Single-threaded, no EB, dark for 95 days, and Medius competitive threat unresolved. Re-engage or disqualify within 14 days.

competitive_position.summary: Medius P2P system purchased September 2025 for Intake and AP, weeks after Zycus discovery call, and may have eliminated the opportunity. Cavendish has been silent for 95 days despite rep outreach to clarify competitive impact. If the deal is still live, the wedge is 3PL warehouse invoice automation, supplier risk, and agentic AI—areas where Medius is not the leader and Ahmed confirmed pain remains unaddressed. No other competitors named.

competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus as the specialist for 3PL warehouse invoice automation (Ahmed confirmed on 17 November that warehousing invoice standardization is still pending post-Medius), supplier risk management, and agentic AI cost-savings discovery. Co-exist with Medius (which won intake/AP) rather than replace it. Quantify the 3PL invoice pain (manual hours, error cost) and demonstrate ROI for the warehouse use case Medius does not cover. Escalate to TMO office and CFO to position Zycus as the Phase II warehousing and risk layer on top of Medius core P2P.

If no reply within 10 business days, escalate to the deal owner's manager for an executive check-in with Ahmed's VP (Mark Alward, VP Business Transformation & Supply Chain, or equivalent). Frame the re-engagement around the unaddressed warehousing pain (3PL invoice chaos) and the agentic AI cost-savings opportunity, not around Medius. If the deal is truly dead, disqualify it and move resources to live opportunities.

items.quote: Please, send Affany a few use cases... Send one or two for each of these modules, and try to make it in your industry... Use cases should include warehouse management scenarios, particularly for third-party logistics integration.
items.requirement: Share one to two tailored industry use cases per module (supplier risk, invoicing, spend analysis, warehouse management, particularly 3PL integration).

items.quote: Send example ROI model results and the data sheet to be filled out for business case development. Zycus to share an example ROI model demonstrating conservative cost-savings spectrum based on recent projects.
items.requirement: Provide an example ROI model demonstrating conservative cost-savings spectrum and the data sheet to be filled out.

items.quote: If you can do something like that, even on a very high level, that would be awesome. Because like that, I would instead of me coming up with some assumptions... based on previous experience with other stuff, I would have something like reality.
items.requirement: Create a high-level strawman implementation roadmap outlining phase 1, phase 2, and phase 3 milestones with resource and cost estimates.

items.quote: Set up a shared Showpad repository... and send access invitations to attendees. Will resend the link to Showpad, which contains use cases and the latest PowerPoint presentation on agentic AI dynamics.
items.requirement: Establish a shared Showpad repository containing use cases, Gartner/Forrester reports, Zycus solution materials, ROI data sheet, sample ROI model, and agentic AI PowerPoint.

items.commitment: Execute NDA and return signed copy to Zycus (Cavendish legal to review and sign).
items.deliverable: Execute NDA and return signed copy to Zycus (Cavendish legal to review and sign).
items.grounding_quote: Ahmed requested NDA before sharing ROI data. Kevin committed to check for existing NDA and send one. Ahmed: 'If you don't mind, I would prefer to use our paper.' Kevin: 'I will have checked to make sure we don't have one already... then you could send it.'

items.commitment: Complete and return the ROI data sheet to Zycus so the ROI model can be built.
items.deliverable: Complete and return the ROI data sheet to Zycus so the ROI model can be built.
items.grounding_quote: Kevin: 'I'm gonna send you the data sheet that we would need filled out... give me the data back once you've populated it as best as you can.' Ahmed agreed to review and return it.

items.commitment: Share 2026 IT and business resource availability with Zycus to inform phased roadmap planning.
items.deliverable: Share 2026 IT and business resource availability with Zycus to inform phased roadmap planning.
items.grounding_quote: Ahmed: 'The plan really for me is to tune in, understand the IT resources for 2026... see where we can fit this based on our resources... once I'll get that information from you, depending on how much hours are needed from IT, how much hours are needed from the business...'

items.commitment: Clarify whether Medius purchase (September 2025, Intake and AP) eliminates or reduces the scope for Zycus S2P solution.
items.deliverable: Clarify whether Medius purchase (September 2025, Intake and AP) eliminates or reduces the scope for Zycus S2P solution.
items.grounding_quote: Rep: 'Have emailed to get a sense on whether this is a live opp or if the Medius purchase negates the need for a Zycus solution. Have reached out to several others including Harshad to see if he can get any intel from Deloitte on whether this means our opp is nullified.' No buyer reply received.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Agentic AI (ANA) is listed in Products__c but was only briefly mentioned on the 17 November call as a future topic ('agentic AI and how that potentially can find surface cost savings, workflow efficiencies'). Ahmed confirmed interest in automation and AI in demand planning but stated logistics and warehousing are 'still a little bit off site' for AI. No AI use case was demonstrated or quantified. Under-positioned given the buyer's data-lake and automation initiatives.

items.quote: Interest was expressed in strengthening supplier management, risk management... Supplier management and risk management capabilities are currently insufficient and identified as high-value areas for Zycus support.
items.criterion: Supplier management and risk management capabilities

items.quote: For warehousing, we don't have any standardization in terms of invoices and categories... We only receive them by email... it's all messed up... very manual. Warehousing invoicing processes remain highly manual, creating inefficiencies and accuracy challenges.
items.criterion: 3PL warehouse invoice automation (standardization, email-to-cloud ingestion, trend analysis, approval workflows)

items.quote: ROI modeling capabilities were welcomed as a valuable tool for financial justification and business-case support. Cavendish Farms seeks ROI modeling that uses internal data rather than industry averages for more accurate business cases.
items.source: Avoma call 16 September 2025, Ahmed Affany and Julie Michaud
items.criterion: ROI modeling using internal data (not industry averages) for business-case support

items.quote: We're working on data lake, data hydration... Deloitte is very involved. They are with us in this transformation journey from data lake, data hydration. They help us support some of the use cases... we might need to involve them as well because they understand our data infrastructure.
items.criterion: Integration with data lake, hydration initiatives, and Deloitte-led transformation

items.quote: They purchased Medius for Intake and AP last September. responded they purchased a P2P system last year. Medius EDI procurement system implemented weeks ago.
items.source: SF Next_Step__c 26 May 2026; Avoma call 17 November 2025
items.criterion: Intake and AP automation (covered by Medius as of September 2025)

items.quote: We're going at it use case by use case. We started the project... control tower... end to end value driven supply chain... If you can do something like that, even on a very high level, that would be awesome.
items.criterion: Use-case-by-use-case evaluation approach and phased roadmap

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong fit on the articulated pain (supplier/risk management, 3PL invoice automation, spend fragmentation, ROI modeling with internal data, data-lake integration). Medius purchase in September 2025 may have satisfied intake and AP needs, reducing scope. Buyer has been silent for 95 days; fit is unconfirmed post-Medius.

hard.ais_why:
• Kevin walked Affany through Zycus's agentic AI launch materials and use cases, with the transcript noting plans to 'leverage agentic AI to identify cost savings and workflow efficiencies in warehousing and transportation.'
• Customer is running a Control Tower / data-lake program with Deloitte and confirmed AI and automation are in demand planning, though production and logistics remain manual - receptive but not yet AI-led.
• Two substantive recorded calls plus monthly cadence; AI is a Zycus-led narrative the customer is open to, not a Cavendish-driven mandate, keeping confidence Medium.
hard.next_step:
5/26MF - update from they purchased Medius for Intake and AP last September.  Have emailed Tyson Woods, Procurement Director to get a sense on whether this is a live opp or if the Medius purchase negates the need for a Zycus solution.  

5/25MF - emailing attendees for the SCC conference, Adrian Mallett, (buyer) responded they purchased a P2P system last year.   Have reached out to several others including Harshad to see if he can get any intel from Deloitte on whether this means our opp is nullified.  

2.13.26 KC:

  • Invited Ahmed to Pinnacle a 2nd time
  • Rescheduling meeting for week of 2.16

 

1.19.26 KC: Meeting rescheduled for 2.11 @ 11am PST,
